short_description_of_the_course.label: Short Description of the Course
short_description_of_the_course.value:
To provide a firm foundation in skills and key concepts essential for programming fundamentally in Python which is the main language used in the programme, while introducing also to Java syntax.
To provide a thorough introduction to problem solving and Python programming, giving a basis for study in topics such as algorithms and data structures, object-oriented design, and human computer interaction.
To develop skills, required for implementing solutions to well-specified programming problems, which could then be transferred to other programming languages like Java.

timetable_and_length_and_frequency_of_te.label: Timetable and length and frequency of teaching sessions
timetable_and_length_and_frequency_of_te.value:
Three lectures, one tutorial and one lab session per week. Special timetable in week 1.
.

course_aims.label: Course Aims
course_aims.value:
To provide a firm foundation in skills and key concepts essential for programming fundamentally in Python which is the main language used in the programme, while also introducing to Java syntax.
To provide a thorough introduction to problem solving and Python programming, giving a basis for study in topics such as algorithms and data structures, object-oriented design, and human computer interaction.
To develop skills, required for implementing solutions to well-specified programming problems, which could then be transferred to other programming languages like Java.

intended_learning_outcomes_of_course.label: Intended Learning Outcomes of Course

formative_assessment_feedback.label: Formative Assessment & Feedback
formative_assessment_feedback.value: Written feedback on summatively assessed coursework. Verbal feedback on tutorial and lab exercises (formatively assessed coursework); verbal feedback during class, tutorial and lab sessions.

additional_relevant_information.label: Additional Relevant Information
additional_relevant_information.value:
The following list of topics gives an indication (though not necessarily an exact breakdown) of the likely content of the course:

Introduction to Programming
Integrated Development Environment
Variables, types, expressions, assignment
int, char, float, double, long
Helper objects, instance variables, constructors
Exceptions
Strings
String formatting
String tokenisation
StringBuilder
Arithmetic
Limited precision
Overflow
Program development
Booleans and boolean operators
Conditionals
The ? operator
Iteration
For loops
While loops
Nested loops
Functions/Methods
Dividing responsibility among functions
Methods, constructors, garbage collection
Stack, heap, shallow / deep copying, garbage collection
Graphical user Interfaces
Layout managers
Events and action listeners
Model-view controller architecture
GUI graphics
GUI components
Console and file I/O
Arrays
Arrays of primitive types
Simple array algorithms
Arrays of objects
Sorting arrays, 2D arrays
Recursion
